description:
## Context

ISS-001: a field report on 52 production agents surfaced concrete agent-prompt design patterns our prompt-engineering skills should teach/enforce — explicit "what you do NOT do" lists, source-quality/output-structure labels, named pattern lists (with the why), a quality self-check, specific inputs, and one-agent-one-job scope.

Audit found patterns 5 (specific inputs) and 6 (narrow scope) already covered (Rung 1; Rung 8 single-shot boundary). The substance is patterns 1/2/3/4, two of which are in tension with existing principles: named prohibitions vs. anti-patterns.md "say what to do", and an in-prompt self-check vs. the "don't ask the LLM to do what code should" north star.

## Desired behavior

Fold the patterns in without bulldozing the tensions: reconcile prohibition guidance (vague negatives bad; specific named failure-mode prohibitions good), add a Guardrails technique rung (named prohibitions, enforced output-structure labels, named pattern lists, a guarded self-check), and teach the eval-driven improve loop to detect fabrication/filler so it routes to the new rung.

## Acceptance criteria

- prompt-engineering-author: anti-patterns.md reconciles positive-instruction vs named prohibition by *specificity*; techniques.md gains Rung 3 "Guardrails: name the failure modes" (renumbered ladder, cross-refs fixed); rewrite-procedure.md + SKILL.md thread it through.
- The self-check guidance is scoped to genuine-judgment bars; deterministic checks stay in code/evals (north star preserved).
- prompt-engineering-improve: improve_step.py THEME_KEYWORDS gains a `fabrication` theme (+ filler under tone_style) with offline tests; diagnosis.md maps fabrication/filler/self-check-drift to the Guardrails rung and bumps fabrication in the priority order.
- `skill_lint.py` clean; prompt-engineering-improve offline suite green.
- ISS-001 linked (relatedTickets) and given a phase.

## Provenance

ISS-001 (discovered 2026-06-20). Source: Reddit field report on awesome-copilot-chat-agents.
title: Fold ISS-001 agent-prompt patterns into prompt-engineering skills
